description:
Extend descr_to_materialize with tuple and empty-list arms.

## Tuple

`fold_make_tuple` (src/reducer.rs:342) produces `Descr::tuple_of(elems)` when all args are literal. is_scalar_literal at ir_reducer.rs:500 currently rejects it (`d.tuples.iter().any(|c| !c.pos.is_empty())`). Materializer arm: push `Stmt::Let(v, Prim::MakeTuple(elem_vars))`. Element vars reused from env.

## Empty list

Post-fz-s9y, `[]` is `EMPTY_LIST = 0x8`, a zero-alloc sentinel like nil. Add an arm that pushes `Stmt::Let(v, Prim::MakeList(vec![], None))` for an empty-list Descr. Alternatively a `Const::EmptyList` variant could be added (see Const enum at src/fz_ir.rs:119) — pick whichever feels less invasive after looking at both shapes.

## Verify

- New minimal fixture: tuple-returning fn called with literals → should Consume end-to-end.
- New minimal fixture (or existing): empty-list-returning fn with literal args → Consumes.
- closure_typed_captures outcomes already cleaner from .2; .3 shouldn't regress.

## Out of scope

Non-empty list literal folding. `fold_prim` skips `Prim::MakeList` at src/reducer.rs:188 because `list_of(elem)` loses length info. Tracked separately as the L1 follow-up.

## Blocked by

fz-f88.2
title: fz-RS.3 — materializer tuple_lit + empty-list arms
